decisions.decision: Use centroid (average vertex position) instead of bounding-box center as rotation pivot
decisions.rationale: Centroid = center of mass for uniform density; rotation around COM keeps the model stable on the build plate

decisions.decision: WASM returns clean positions (no degenerate triangles) to keep positions/normals/areas in sync
decisions.rationale: mesh::precompute_mesh filters zero-area triangles; returning 'flat' caused mismatch between positions array and normals/areas arrays

decisions.decision: Multi-worker architecture splitting directions across navigator.hardwareConcurrency - 1 workers
decisions.rationale: Parallel scoring gives ~4x speedup on quad-core; each worker processes a contiguous dirStart/dirCount slice

decisions.decision: Decimate both positions and normals/areas to ~12K elements for scoring
decisions.rationale: Full 500K model x 400 candidates = 200M iterations; 12K = 50x fewer, rank order preserved

decisions.decision: Compose full candidate quaternion as qYaw * qAlign(dir, -Y)
decisions.rationale: computeDefaultYaw only returns yaw (rotation around candidate axis); need to first align the candidate direction to -Y for correct display and lift

next_action: Verify the fix: load an STL at localhost:5173, check that all candidates sit on the build plate (lowest point at y=0) and overhang colors correspond to the shadow the model would cast on the plate
context_notes: The user identified that candidates appear below the build plate plane. Root cause: the quaternion from computeDefaultYaw only contains yaw (rotation around the candidate direction axis), not the alignment of that direction to -Y. Fix: compose qFull = qYaw * quaternionAlign(dir, (0,-1,0)) in computeSlice. Also fixed: WASM returning degenerate triangles in positions but not in normals (caused splattered overhang colors), and switched from bbox-center to centroid for rotation pivot. Vite dev server running at localhost:5173.
